content.text:
cd /workspace/YatCC && python3 -c "
[?2004l[?2004h# Let me trace through the line mapping logic
[?2004l[?2004h# The preprocessed file has 10 lines (plus empty line at end)
[?2004l[?2004h# ANTLR gives us line 8 for 'int'
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Our code:
[?2004l[?2004h# 1. Parse each line of raw content
[?2004l[?2004h# 2. For each line, check if it's a line directive
[?2004l[?2004h# 3. If it is, update currentFile and currentLine
[?2004l[?2004h# 4. Push {currentFile, currentLine} to lineMap
[?2004l[?2004h# 5. Increment currentLine
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Let me trace through:
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 1: '# 1 \"/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c\"'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 2: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 1'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 3: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 3'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 4: '# 389 \"<built-in>\" 3'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=389, currentFile='<built-in>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 389})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=390
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 5: '# 1 \"<command line>\" 1'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<command line>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<command line>', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 6: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 2'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7: '# 1 \"/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c\" 2'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Line 8: 'int main(){'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - Not a directive
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=3
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# So lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
[?2004l[?2004h# ANTLR gives us line 8 for 'int'
[?2004l[?2004h# We look up lineMap[8-1] = lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
[?2004l[?2004h# So we output line 2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# But the expected output shows line 1 for 'int'
[?2004l[?2004h# The issue is that the line directive on line 7 says:
[?2004l[?2004h# 'The NEXT line is line 1 of the source file'
[?2004l[?2004h# So line 8 should be source line 1, not line 2
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# The bug is that we're incrementing currentLine AFTER pushing to lineMap
[?2004l[?2004h# For non-directive lines, we should NOT increment currentLine
[?2004l[?2004h# We should only increment currentLine for non-directive lines
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Wait, actually the issue is different
[?2004l[?2004h# The line directive says 'line 1 of source file X'
[?2004l[?2004h# This means the NEXT line (line 8) is line 1 of the source
[?2004l[?2004h# So lineMap[7] should be {'...', 1}, not {'...', 2}
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# But our code gives lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
[?2004l[?2004h# Because we increment currentLine after pushing
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# The fix is: for non-directive lines, don't increment currentLine
[?2004l[?2004h# Only increment currentLine for directive lines if they don't already set it
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Actually, let me re-read the line directive format
[?2004l[?2004h# # line_number \"filename\" flags
[?2004l[?2004h# This means: the NEXT line in the preprocessed file corresponds to line_number of filename
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# So:
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7 directive: next line (line 8) is line 1 of '...'
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 8: source line 1
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 9: source line 2
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 10: source line 3
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Our code:
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7: directive sets currentLine=1
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7: lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7: currentLine++ -> currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 8: lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})  <- WRONG! Should be {'...', 1}
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# The fix: don't increment currentLine for non-directive lines
[?2004l[?2004h# Only increment currentLine when we encounter a new directive
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Actually, the correct logic is:
[?2004l[?2004h# For each line in the preprocessed file:
[?2004l[?2004h#   If it's a line directive:
[?2004l[?2004h#     currentLine = directive_line_number
[?2004l[?2004h#     currentFile = directive_filename
[?2004l[?2004h#   lineMap.push_back({currentFile, currentLine})
[?2004l[?2004h#   currentLine++  # Only for non-directive lines? No, always increment
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Wait, the issue is that the directive line itself doesn't have source code
[?2004l[?2004h# The directive line is just metadata
[?2004l[?2004h# The NEXT line is the actual source code
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# So the mapping should be:
[?2004l[?2004h# lineMap[i] = source info for preprocessed line i+1
[?2004l[?2004h# But the directive line doesn't have source code
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Actually, I think the simplest fix is:
[?2004l[?2004h# When we see a line directive, set currentLine to the directive's line number
[?2004l[?2004h# Then for the NEXT line (which is the actual source), currentLine will be correct
[?2004l[?2004h# Because we increment currentLine after pushing
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Let me trace through again with this understanding:
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 1: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 1
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 2: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 2
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h# ...
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 7: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 7
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=2
[?2004l[?2004h# Line 8: 'int main(){'
[?2004l[?2004h#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})  <- This is for preprocessed line 8
[?2004l[?2004h#   - currentLine=3
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# So lineMap[7] = {'...', 2} for preprocessed line 8
[?2004l[?2004h# But preprocessed line 8 should be source line 1
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# The issue is that we're incrementing currentLine after pushing
[?2004l[?2004h# For non-directive lines, we should NOT increment currentLine
[?2004l[?2004h# Because the line directive already tells us the line number for the next line
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Actually, I think the correct logic is:
[?2004l[?2004h# For each line in the preprocessed file:
[?2004l[?2004h#   If it's a line directive:
[?2004l[?2004h#     currentLine = directive_line_number
[?2004l[?2004h#     currentFile = directive_filename
[?2004l[?2004h#   lineMap.push_back({currentFile, currentLine})
[?2004l[?2004h#   # Don't increment currentLine here
[?2004l[?2004h#   # The next line will use the same currentLine (if it's a directive) or currentLine+1 (if it's not)
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Wait, that doesn't work either
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Let me think about this differently
[?2004l[?2004h# The line directive says: 'The NEXT line is line N of file F'
[?2004l[?2004h# So when we see a directive on line D, we know:
[?2004l[?2004h#   lineMap[D] = {F, N}  <- This is wrong, lineMap[D] is for line D+1
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Actually, I think the simplest approach is:
[?2004l[?2004h# Don't build lineMap during parsing
[?2004l[?2004h# Instead, build it after parsing by looking at the raw content
[?2004l[?2004h# For each preprocessed line, find the most recent line directive
[?2004l[?2004h# And use that to determine the source file and line
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004h# Or even simpler: just use the raw content to find the line directive for each token
[?2004l[?2004h[?2004l[?2004hprint('Need to fix line mapping logic')"
[?2004lNeed to fix line mapping logic
[?2004h

observation.command:
cd /workspace/YatCC && python3 -c "
# Let me trace through the line mapping logic
# The preprocessed file has 10 lines (plus empty line at end)
# ANTLR gives us line 8 for 'int'

# Our code:
# 1. Parse each line of raw content
# 2. For each line, check if it's a line directive
# 3. If it is, update currentFile and currentLine
# 4. Push {currentFile, currentLine} to lineMap
# 5. Increment currentLine

# Let me trace through:
# Line 1: '# 1 \"/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c\"'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 2: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 1'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 3: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 3'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 4: '# 389 \"<built-in>\" 3'
#   - Directive: currentLine=389, currentFile='<built-in>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 389})
#   - currentLine=390

# Line 5: '# 1 \"<command line>\" 1'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<command line>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<command line>', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 6: '# 1 \"<built-in>\" 2'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 7: '# 1 \"/workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c\" 2'
#   - Directive: currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
#   - currentLine=2

# Line 8: 'int main(){'
#   - Not a directive
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})
#   - currentLine=3

# So lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
# ANTLR gives us line 8 for 'int'
# We look up lineMap[8-1] = lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
# So we output line 2

# But the expected output shows line 1 for 'int'
# The issue is that the line directive on line 7 says:
# 'The NEXT line is line 1 of the source file'
# So line 8 should be source line 1, not line 2

# The bug is that we're incrementing currentLine AFTER pushing to lineMap
# For non-directive lines, we should NOT increment currentLine
# We should only increment currentLine for non-directive lines

# Wait, actually the issue is different
# The line directive says 'line 1 of source file X'
# This means the NEXT line (line 8) is line 1 of the source
# So lineMap[7] should be {'...', 1}, not {'...', 2}

# But our code gives lineMap[7] = {'...', 2}
# Because we increment currentLine after pushing

# The fix is: for non-directive lines, don't increment currentLine
# Only increment currentLine for directive lines if they don't already set it

# Actually, let me re-read the line directive format
# # line_number \"filename\" flags
# This means: the NEXT line in the preprocessed file corresponds to line_number of filename

# So:
# Line 7 directive: next line (line 8) is line 1 of '...'
# Line 8: source line 1
# Line 9: source line 2
# Line 10: source line 3

# Our code:
# Line 7: directive sets currentLine=1
# Line 7: lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})
# Line 7: currentLine++ -> currentLine=2
# Line 8: lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})  <- WRONG! Should be {'...', 1}

# The fix: don't increment currentLine for non-directive lines
# Only increment currentLine when we encounter a new directive

# Actually, the correct logic is:
# For each line in the preprocessed file:
#   If it's a line directive:
#     currentLine = directive_line_number
#     currentFile = directive_filename
#   lineMap.push_back({currentFile, currentLine})
#   currentLine++  # Only for non-directive lines? No, always increment

# Wait, the issue is that the directive line itself doesn't have source code
# The directive line is just metadata
# The NEXT line is the actual source code

# So the mapping should be:
# lineMap[i] = source info for preprocessed line i+1
# But the directive line doesn't have source code

# Actually, I think the simplest fix is:
# When we see a line directive, set currentLine to the directive's line number
# Then for the NEXT line (which is the actual source), currentLine will be correct
# Because we increment currentLine after pushing

# Let me trace through again with this understanding:
# Line 1: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 1
#   - currentLine=2
# Line 2: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='<built-in>'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'<built-in>', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 2
#   - currentLine=2
# ...
# Line 7: directive sets currentLine=1, currentFile='...'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 1})  <- This is for preprocessed line 7
#   - currentLine=2
# Line 8: 'int main(){'
#   - lineMap.push_back({'...', 2})  <- This is for preprocessed line 8
#   - currentLine=3

# So lineMap[7] = {'...', 2} for preprocessed line 8
# But preprocessed line 8 should be source line 1

# The issue is that we're incrementing currentLine after pushing
# For non-directive lines, we should NOT increment currentLine
# Because the line directive already tells us the line number for the next line

# Actually, I think the correct logic is:
# For each line in the preprocessed file:
#   If it's a line directive:
#     currentLine = directive_line_number
#     currentFile = directive_filename
#   lineMap.push_back({currentFile, currentLine})
#   # Don't increment currentLine here
#   # The next line will use the same currentLine (if it's a directive) or currentLine+1 (if it's not)

# Wait, that doesn't work either

# Let me think about this differently
# The line directive says: 'The NEXT line is line N of file F'
# So when we see a directive on line D, we know:
#   lineMap[D] = {F, N}  <- This is wrong, lineMap[D] is for line D+1

# Actually, I think the simplest approach is:
# Don't build lineMap during parsing
# Instead, build it after parsing by looking at the raw content
# For each preprocessed line, find the most recent line directive
# And use that to determine the source file and line

# Or even simpler: just use the raw content to find the line directive for each token

print('Need to fix line mapping logic')"
